title: armeria vulgaris var. alpina
intro: Armeria vulgaris var. alpina, also known as Alpine Sea Thrift, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Alpine Sea Thrift can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is sometimes used topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
